result:
Build is clean (only pre-existing unused-import warnings). The fix is complete.

## Report

**Root cause:** `crates/lua-parse/src/lib.rs` `cg_discharge_vars` for `ExprKind::Indexed` (line 1426) freed the table and key registers in the wrong order. `cg_free_reg_if_temp` (line 1753) only decrements `freereg` when `reg == freereg - 1`; called with the lower register first, it silently no-ops. So after compiling `a[n-3]` where `a` is in R2 and `n-3` in R3 (freereg=R4), only R3 got freed — R2 stayed "live" with the table value, and the subsequent GETTABLE result was patched into a fresh register R3 instead of overwriting R2. R2 then leaked through the next call's argument list, producing the spurious "table" value seen in `__index` results.

**Fix:** In the `ExprKind::Indexed` arm, free the higher register first (matches C-Lua's `freeregs`):
```rust
if idx_reg > t_reg { free(idx_reg); free(t_reg); }
else                { free(t_reg); free(idx_reg); }
```

**New state:** The minimal repro (`a[i*3]` through a 3-deep `__index` chain ending in a function returning `a[n-3]+4`) now passes all 11 iterations and prints "OK". `cargo build -p lua-cli` is green. I did not re-run the full events.lua test per the instructions — the mega-loop will re-test it.

tool_input.description: Run lua-rs binary on test source

tool_input.command:
target/debug/lua-rs 'do
  local a;
  a = setmetatable({}, {__index = setmetatable({},
                     {__index = setmetatable({},
                     {__index = function (_,n) print("FN called n=", n); return a[n-3]+4, "lixo" end})})})
  a[0] = 20
  local v = a[3]
  print("v=", v)
end' >/tmp/out 2>&1; tail -50 /tmp/out
